source_jobs.title: Principal Rendering Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Studio:

We are a video game development studio with offices in Guildford, UK and Utrecht, Netherlands. Sitting within the heart of thriving communities of game developers and publishers, we focus on quality and excellence, and our partners and players are always at the front of everything we do.

Games are not just about gameplay. We believe in the importance of balancing every single aspect of a game that you, as a player will interact with; what you see, how you play and how it makes you feel.

It couldn’t be a more exciting time to join us as we embark on a new unannounced project, pushing the boundaries of all aspects of the game to create a truly AAA experience.




The Role

As our Principal Rendering Engineer you will be working closely with our Technical Director and Engineering team to develop and optimise the rendering and graphics technology we implement on our AAA projects.

Your main focus will be collaborating with our team of engineers, artists, and technical artists to design, implement, and optimize rendering features. You will be at the forefront of creating visually stunning and immersive experiences for our players - we want our games to inspire and amaze.

As our Principal Rendering Engineer, you will:

 - Develop and optimize rendering features

 - Implement shaders and lighting systems

 - Collaborate with artists to achieve exceptional visual quality

 - Optimize performance and memory usage

 - Maintain and enhance the rendering pipeline

 - Research and implement cutting-edge rendering techniques

 - Provide technical guidance to the team



Requirements

What we’re looking to see on your CV:

 - Significant experience in real-time rendering and graphics programming

 - Proficiency in C++ and shader programming languages (e.g., HLSL, GLSL)

 - Strong understanding of rendering algorithms and techniques

 - Experience with Unreal Engine or other modern game engines

 - Knowledge of performance profiling and optimization techniques

 - Excellent problem-solving and debugging skills

 - Strong communication and teamwork skills

 - Previous experience shipping AAA titles is a plus



Bonus points if you have;

 - At least one, or two extra languages under your belt

 - Previous experience in building large scale, realtime, multiplayer games



Benefits

What you can expect from us:

 - International AAA game projects

 - Access to cutting-edge hardware

 - Flexible/hybrid working - we're happy to discuss what this looks like for this role, but our ideal candidate would be able to join us regularly in Guildford

 - Great increasing holiday benefits including Christmas Shutdown

 - Discretionary bonus scheme

 - Private Healthcare Plan including dental and optical support, mental health, online GP

 - Pension Plan

 - Life Assurance

 - Flu Jab Vouchers

 - Regular Team Incentives and Social Events

 - Access to a well-stocked fridge and snack cupboard

 - Opportunities to work with our Netherlands office

source_jobs.title: Talent Pool
source_jobs.description:
Whether you’re fresh on the scene up to a decorated veteran, there’s a place for all at Stellar. We are committed to the inclusion of all and making incredible games with a truly diverse team. Check out our openings below and join us on our mission to create and amaze.



Can’t see your dream role? We are always on the look for new talent, drop your CV and cover letter into our talent pool below to keep in touch with our recruitment team.

source_jobs.title: Senior Vehicle Handling Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Studio:

We are a video game development studio with offices in Guildford, UK and Utrecht, Netherlands. Sitting within the heart of thriving communities of game developers and publishers, we focus on quality and excellence, and our partners and players are always at the front of everything we do.

Games are not just about gameplay. We believe in the importance of balancing every single aspect of a game that you, as a player will interact with; what you see, how you play and how it makes you feel.

It couldn’t be a more exciting time to join us as we embark on a new unannounced project, pushing the boundaries of all aspects of the game to create a truly AAA experience.




The Role

We're looking for a Senior Engineer to join our Vehicle Handling & Physics team, working to bridge the gap between Design and Code to create, implement and refine vehicle handling systems to create truly immersive, authentic, and responsive driving experiences.

Taking ownership of the Physics system, you will implement the code that shapes how vehicles feel and respond in the hands of players.

Our teams are really pushing the limits of what’s possible – if you’re excited by ambitious projects, complex technical challenges and solving problems, this could be the role for you.

As a Vehicle Handling Engineer, you will:

 - Take ownership of our Physics and vehicle systems

 - Develop core driving mechanics that create unforgettable driving experiences

 - Work closely with the Design team to translate 'feel' into scalable systems



What we're looking to see on your CV

 - Strong C++ and Unreal Engine experience

 - Experience integrating and owning Physics systems (e.g. PhysX, Havok or Chaos)

 - Solid understanding of 3D Maths, collision detection, vehicle handling and multithreaded programming

Bonus points if you have:

 - Shipped racing titles under your belt

 - Previous experience building large scale, real-time, multiplayer games



Benefits

What you can expect from us:

 - International AAA game projects

 - Flexible/hybrid working (we work 3 days per week on site)

 - Great increasing holiday benefits including Christmas Shutdown

 - Discretionary bonus scheme

 - Private Healthcare Plan including dental and optical support, mental health, online GP

 - Pension Plan

 - Life Assurance

 - Flu Jab Vouchers

 - Regular Team Incentives and Social Events

 - Access to a well-stocked fridge and snack cupboard

 - Opportunities to work with our Netherlands office

source_jobs.title: Senior Gameplay Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Studio

We are a video game development studio with offices in Guildford, UK and Utrecht, Netherlands. Sitting within the heart of thriving communities of game developers and publishers, we focus on quality and excellence, and our partners and players are always at the front of everything we do.



Games are not just about gameplay. We believe in the importance of balancing every single aspect of a game that you, as a player will interact with; what you see, how you play and how it makes you feel.



It couldn’t be a more exciting time to join us as we embark on new unannounced projects, pushing the boundaries of all aspects of the game to create AAA experiences.



The Role

We’re now looking for a Senior Gameplay Engineer to join us as we create memorable gameplay experiences on our AAA titles. This is a hands-on role that will work closely with our Lead Gameplay Engineer, Technical Directors and Designers to craft engaging gameplay mechanics, controls and systems – owning the process from the initial design concepts right the way through to implementation, with a focus on quality, performance and scalability.



Great gameplay is created by Engineers and designers that really care about how games feel in the hands of players. If you enjoy problem solving and seeing how your code and systems interact with our players, we’d love to hear from you.



As a Senior Gameplay Engineer, you will:

 - Design, implement and own core gameplay mechanics, controls, and systems, working with Designers to prototype features and ensure successful integration

 - Write clean, robust and efficient code, following best practices and coding standards.

 - Debug and optimize gameplay systems to ensure continued stability and performance.

 - Contribute to the development of tools and workflows to improve productivity and efficiency.

 - Review code, mentor and advise other mid-level Gameplay Engineers in the team.

 - Participate in sprint and milestone planning and production reviews.



Requirements

What we’re looking to see on your CV:

 - Experience in a Senior Gameplay Engineer position or similar.

 - Proficient in C++ and familiar with both object-oriented and data-oriented programming.

 - Previous experience owning a feature on a title

 - Strong knowledge of game engines, such as Unreal Engine or Unity.

 - Demonstrable knowledge and understanding of 3D Maths and Physics.

 - Experience with source control systems and collaborative development.

 - Good problem-solving and debugging skills.

 - Excellent communication and team collaboration skills.

 - Passion for video games and familiarity with various game genres.

 - Ability to work in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.



Bonus points for:

 - More than one shipped title under your belt

 - Experience with multiplayer or networked gameplay systems

 - Familiarity with other Engineering disciplines like Animation, UI, or AI



Benefits

What you can expect from us:



Our UK Colleagues receive:

 - Flexible hybrid working (we work 3 days per week on-site)

 - Holiday Accrual Scheme

 - International AAA game projects;

 - Access to cutting-edge hardware;

 - Discretionary bonus scheme

 - Private Healthcare Plan incl. Dental and Eye care

 - Pension Plan

 - Life Assurance

 - Employee Assistance Programme - Wellness Support

 - Regular Team Incentives and Social Events



Our NL Colleagues receive:

 - International AAA game projects

 - Access to cutting-edge hardware

 - Flexible/hybrid working

 - International working opportunities with our Guildford Studio

 - Christmas Shutdown

 - Holiday Allowance

 - Discretionary bonus scheme

 - 13th Salary Scheme (Vakantiegeld)

 - Central Utrecht office

 - Access to a well-stocked fridge and snack cupboard

 - Regular Team Incentives and Social Events

source_jobs.title: Rendering Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Studio:

We are a video game development studio with offices in Guildford, UK and Utrecht, Netherlands. Sitting within the heart of thriving communities of game developers and publishers, we focus on quality and excellence, and our partners and players are always at the front of everything we do.



Games are not just about gameplay. We believe in the importance of balancing every single aspect of a game that you, as a player will interact with; what you see, how you play and how it makes you feel.



It couldn’t be a more exciting time to join us as we embark on a new unannounced project, pushing the boundaries of all aspects of the game to create a truly AAA experience.




The Role

As Stellar Entertainment’s Rendering Engineer, you will be working closely with our wider Rendering and Engineering team to help develop and optimise the rendering and graphics technology we implement on our AAA projects.

Your main focus will be collaborating with our team of engineers, artists, and technical artists to design, implement, and optimize rendering features. You will be involved in the creation of visually stunning and immersive experiences for our players - we want our games to inspire and amaze.

As our Rendering Engineer, you will:

 - Work under direction to develop and optimize rendering features

 - Assist with the implementation shaders and lighting systems

 - Coordinate across teams to achieve exceptional visual quality

 - Work towards optimising performance and memory usage

 - Collaborate with colleagues to maintain and enhance the rendering pipeline

 - Research and implement cutting-edge rendering techniques

 - Work with colleagues to identify bugs and solutions



Requirements

What we’re looking to see on your CV:

 - Experience in a Rendering role in the gaming industry, working in Unreal Engine

 - Strong programming skills in C++, and ability to demonstrate your 3D maths expertise

 - Good knowledge of rendering techniques and algorithms, and experience with shader programming and real-time lighting

 - Understanding of performance optimization techniques

 - Unreal Engine familiarity

 - Ability to work coordinate across teams

 - A keen interest in for pushing the boundaries of what’s possible visually, and keeping up to date with latest tech developments in the field

Benefits

source_jobs.title: Hard Surface Artist
source_jobs.description:
The Studio:

We are a video game development studio with offices in Guildford, UK and Utrecht, Netherlands. Sitting within the heart of thriving communities of game developers and publishers, we focus on quality and excellence, and our partners and players are always at the front of everything we do.



Games are not just about gameplay. We believe in the importance of balancing every single aspect of a game that you, as a player will interact with; what you see, how you play and how it makes you feel.



It couldn’t be a more exciting time to join us as we embark on a new, unannounced project.




The Role

We are looking for a talented Hard Surface Artist to join our Art team. This is a mid-level role, and will suit someone looking to build on their games industry experience as a Hard Surface Artist.



Working alongside our Lead Hard Surface Artist, you will craft high-quality hard surface assets - from vehicles and weapons to environment props and architectural elements, that bring our game world to life. You'll help define and maintain the visual bar for the project while collaborating across disciplines to deliver polished assets and content.



As our Hard Surface Artist you will:



 - Create high and low poly hard surface models (weapons, vehicles, props, structures, and environmental assets) to a high standard of quality.

 - Produce clean, optimised topology and UV layouts appropriate for real-time game engines.

 - Author PBR texture sets using industry-standard tools, ensuring assets are physically accurate and visually consistent with the project's art style.

 - Work from concept art and reference material to produce assets that meet design and artistic intent.

 - Integrate assets into the game engine and iterate based on feedback from leads and art directors.

 - Collaborate with environment, character, and technical art teams to ensure seamless asset integration.

 - Contribute to and maintain art documentation, style guides, and pipeline workflows.

 - Participate in art reviews, offering and receiving constructive feedback.

 - Identify and flag technical or artistic issues proactively and propose solutions.





Requirements

What we’re looking to see on your CV and portfolio:

 - Solid games industry experience as a Hard Surface Artist, with at least one shipped title under your belt.

 - A strong portfolio that demonstrates mastery of hard surface modelling and material work across a range of subject matter - showing your process as well as the finished result.

 - Proficiency in Blender, Substance Painter, Substance Designer, and Unreal Engine.

 - A confident grasp of the mid-poly workflow, including baking, ID masking, and efficient asset preparation for real-time pipelines.



Bonus points for:

 - Experience on AAA or mid-tier console/PC titles.

 - Familiarity with Houdini or procedural modelling techniques.

 - Knowledge of Marmoset Toolbag for baking and asset presentation.

 - Experience with photogrammetry pipelines.

 - An interest in game design and how assets function in context.



Benefits

What you can expect from us:

 - Flexible hybrid working (we work 3 days per week on-site)

 - Holiday Accrual Scheme

 - International AAA game projects;

 - Access to cutting-edge hardware;

 - Discretionary bonus scheme

 - Private Healthcare Plan incl. Dental and Eye care

 - Pension Plan

 - Life Assurance

 - Employee Assistance Programme - Wellness Support

 - Regular Team Incentives and Social Events



Our NL Colleagues receive:

 - International AAA game projects

 - Access to cutting-edge hardware

 - Flexible/hybrid working

 - International working opportunities with our Guildford Studio

 - Christmas Shutdown

 - Holiday Allowance

 - Discretionary bonus scheme

 - 13th Salary Scheme (Vakantiegeld)

 - Central Utrecht office

 - Access to a well-stocked fridge and snack cupboard

 - Regular Team Incentives and Social Events

source_jobs.title: Associate Art Director
source_jobs.description:
The Studio

We are a video game development studio with offices in Guildford, UK and Utrecht, Netherlands. Sitting within the heart of thriving communities of game developers and publishers, we focus on quality and excellence, and our partners and players are always at the front of everything we do.



Games are not just about gameplay. We believe in the importance of balancing every single aspect of a game that you, as a player will interact with; what you see, how you play and how it makes you feel.



It couldn’t be a more exciting time to join us as we embark on a new unannounced project, pushing the boundaries of all aspects of the game to create a truly AAA experience.


The Role

Our studio is going from strength to strength and we’re now looking for an Associate Art Director to join us to continue building upon our high-quality art aspirations but also leading a team of high achieving artists. You will collaborate with the Art Director and teams across many disciplines including UI, Hard Surface, World, VFX, Animation, Engineering and Production to define and drive the quality in our artistic vision for our games.



Alongside leveraging your artistic abilities, you’ll also mentor and manage a variety of artists in different disciplines, providing guidance and support to help them reach their full potential. We’re passionate about career development, and our Associate Art Director will play a key role in the progression of our teams. You’ll be a naturally strong communicator and team leader, who can maintain the balance of artistic creativity and technical constraints to help us deliver visually stunning and immersive game experiences.



As our Associate Art Director, you will:



 - Help to refine and sculpt the artistic vision of the game alongside our Art Director and Chief Creative Officer

 - Collaborate across teams to ensure the art direction complements gameplay

 - Lead and mentor our team of talented artists, giving regular feedback and check-ins on the work produced

 - Work to grow the team’s overall capabilities, maintaining and fostering a healthy culture that means everyone is hungry to be class leading across all disciplines.

 - Ensure we are delivering quality and consistency regarding the artwork we are creating for our projects

 - Communicate and celebrate art progress alongside highlighting risks and concerns looking to optimise the maximum potential of the team





Requirements

What we’re looking to see on your CV:

 - Proven experience as an Associate Art Director on released game projects within the AAA space.

 - Exceptional portfolio showcasing a strong understanding of artistic fundamentals, including composition, lighting, and visual storytelling.

 - Proficiency in industry-standard art tools (e.g., Photoshop, Maya, Blender, ZBrush, Substance Suite) and game engines (e.g., Unreal Engine).

 - Strong leadership and mentorship skills, with a track record of guiding and developing artistic talent.

 - Excellent communication skills, with the ability to clearly convey artistic vision and provide constructive feedback across multiple disciplines.

 - Ability to balance creative ambition with production realities and technical constraints to deliver high-quality results on time.

 - Strong understanding of the game development process and the intersection of art and technology in game production.



Bonus Points For

 - Experience working on large-scale, open-world, or multiplayer games.

 - Knowledge of advanced rendering techniques and tools (e.g., shaders, lighting systems).

 - Experience in developing art pipelines and workflows that improve productivity and efficiency.

 - Passion for video games and a strong understanding of the latest industry trends in game art and visual styles.



Benefits

What you can expect from us:

 - International AAA game projects

 - Access to cutting-edge hardware

 - Flexible/hybrid working (we work 3 days per week on site)

 - Great increasing holiday be

source_jobs.title: Senior Systems Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We’re looking for a Senior Systems Engineer to join the Stellar Entertainment mission!



The Studio

We are a video game development studio with offices in Guildford, UK and Utrecht, Netherlands. Sitting within the heart of thriving communities of game developers and publishers, we focus on quality and excellence, and our partners and players are always at the front of everything we do.



Games are not just about gameplay. We believe in the importance of balancing every single aspect of a game that you, as a player will interact with; what you see, how you play and how it makes you feel.



An exciting new project is taking shape at Stellar. You'll be joining a specialist team who are ready to provide technical solutions and optimisations.



The Role

As a Senior Systems Engineer, you will be a part of the core tech team working closely with our graphics, gameplay, engine and tools teams. Day to day this will look like:



 - Writing clean, well-documented C++ code in line with project standards

 - Building scalable, data-driven architectures within engine infrastructure

 - Working with the Lead Systems Engineer to conduct a thorough technical assessment of engine and game systems

 - Collaborating across departments to design and deliver key engine features.

 - Profiling and resolving performance issues across CPU, GPU and memory

 - Ensuring high-quality, maintainable output - from broad system design down to individual functions



Requirements

What we’re looking to see on your CV:

 - Experience in game development through all stages of the process from design to shipping, with strong knowledge of optimisation and porting processes

 - Strong understanding of hardware across different platforms

 - Strong C++ skills, including sound knowledge of multithreading, software design patterns, and systems architecture

 - Familiarity with game engine architecture across multiple engines including proprietary

 - Good problem solving and debugging skills



Bonus points for:

 - Any additional coding languages under your belt

 - Understanding of hardware architecture

 - Knowledge of build systems



Benefits

What you can expect from us:

 - Flexible hybrid working (we work 3 days per week on-site)

 - Holiday Accrual Scheme

 - International AAA game projects;

 - Access to cutting-edge hardware;

 - Discretionary bonus scheme

 - Private Healthcare Plan incl. Dental and Eye care

 - Pension Plan

 - Life Assurance

 - Employee Assistance Programme - Wellness Support

 - Regular Team Incentives and Social Events



We’re on an important journey with our teammates, candidates and industry partners to tackle barriers to equality and promote diversity, inclusion and accessibility across the markets we work in. If you need any support during the interview process or any feedback on our processes, please let us know.



We're unfortunately unable to provide visa sponsorship or relocation support for this role, so you'll need to be based in the UK with the Right to Work here.

source_jobs.title: Optimisation Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We’re looking for an Optimisation Engineer to join the Stellar Entertainment mission!



The Studio

We are a video game development studio with offices in Guildford, UK and Utrecht, Netherlands. Sitting within the heart of thriving communities of game developers and publishers, we focus on quality and excellence, and our partners and players are always at the front of everything we do.



Games are not just about gameplay. We believe in the importance of balancing every single aspect of a game that you, as a player will interact with; what you see, how you play and how it makes you feel.



An exciting new project is taking shape at Stellar. You'll be joining a specialist team who are ready to provide technical solutions and optimisations.



The Role

As an Optimisation Engineer, you will be a part of the core tech team working closely with graphics, gameplay, engine and tools. Day to day you will:



 - Profile and analyse CPU and GPU workloads, identifying performance bottlenecks

 - Define, track, and enforce frame time, memory, and bandwidth budgets across all game systems in collaboration with leads and the Technical Director.

 - Collaborate with artists, designers, and programmers to ensure visual quality

 - Provide hands-on mentoring and code review support to mid-level engineers on performance-sensitive coding practices



Requirements

What we’re looking to see on your CV:

 - Experience in game development through all stages of the process from design to shipping

 - Experience in the games industry, in a performance, platform, or core technology role.

 - Strong C++ skills, including knowledge of multithreading, software design patterns, and systems architecture

 - Experience and understanding of low-level CPU and GPU profiling tools

 - Experience working with large code bases

 - Familiarity with game engine architecture across multiple engines

 - Good problem solving and debugging skills



Bonus points for:

 - Any additional coding languages under your belt

 - Understanding of hardware architecture



Benefits

What you can expect from us:

 - Flexible hybrid working (we work 3 days per week on-site, and have core hours of 10:00 – 16:30)

 - Holiday Accrual Scheme

 - International AAA game projects;

 - Access to cutting-edge hardware;

 - Discretionary bonus scheme

 - Private Healthcare Plan incl. Dental and Eye care

 - Pension Plan

 - Regular Team Incentives and Social Events





We’re on an important journey with our teammates, candidates and industry partners to tackle barriers to equality and promote diversity, inclusion and accessibility across the markets we work in. source_jobs.title: Senior Generalist & Physics Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Studio

We are a video game development studio with offices in Guildford, UK and Utrecht, Netherlands. Sitting within the heart of thriving communities of game developers and publishers, we focus on quality and excellence, and our partners and players are always at the front of everything we do.



Games are not just about gameplay. We believe in the importance of balancing every single aspect of a game that you, as a player will interact with; what you see, how you play and how it makes you feel.



The Role

We have an opening for a Physics Engineer to join our rapidly growing team at Stellar Entertainment to work on an exciting new project.



If creating believable, physically accurate worlds pushing the next level of video games is your passion, then this role could be for you. Reporting to the Lead Physics Engineer and partnering with our Creative Director and gameplay teams, your code will create realism within our high-quality titles.



An exciting new project is taking shape at Stellar. You'll be joining a specialist team who are ready to explore technical innovations and optimisations.



As a Senior Generalist & Physics Engineer, you will:

 - Work across a broad range of disciplines including gameplay, systems, engine and physics to successfully optimise code, ready for target platforms

 - Design and implement vehicle-related systems

 - Profiling, benchmarking, and optimising code across CPU

 - Identifying and resolving performance bottlenecks

 - Collaborating with designers and producers to tune handling feel

 - Working with platform engineers to exploit hardware capabilities and hit performance targets across all target platforms

 - Reviewing and providing feedback on colleagues' code, and contributing to a high engineering standard across the team

 - Picking up unfamiliar systems quickly, getting up to speed, and leaving things better than you found them



Requirements

What we’re looking to see on your CV:

 - Experience in a Senior C++ engineering role, in the games industry. Ideally this will be working on AAA or optimisation projects.

 - Proficient in C++ and familiar with both object-oriented and data-oriented programming.

 - Strong knowledge of game engines

 - Demonstrable knowledge and understanding of 3D Maths and Physics.

 - Experience with source control systems and collaborative development.

 - Good problem-solving and debugging skills.

 - Excellent communication and team collaboration skills.

 - Passion for video games and familiarity with various game genres.

 - Ability to work in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.





Benefits

What you can expect from us:

 - Flexible hybrid working (we work 3 days per week on-site)

 - Holiday Accrual Scheme

 - International AAA game projects;

 - Access to cutting-edge hardware;

 - Discretionary bonus scheme

 - Private Healthcare Plan incl. Dental and Eye care

 - Pension Plan

 - Life Assurance

 - Employee Assistance Programme - Wellness Support

 - Regular Team Incentives and Social Events





We’re on an important journey with our teammates, candidates and industry partners to tackle barriers to equality and promote diversity, inclusion and accessibility across the markets we work in. If you need any support during the interview process or any feedback on our processes, please let us know.



We're unfortunately unable to provide visa sponsorship or relocation support for this role, so you'll need to be based in the UK with the Right to Work here.

source_jobs.title: Senior Producer
source_jobs.description:
The Studio

We are a video game development studio with offices in Guildford, UK and Utrecht, Netherlands. Sitting within the heart of thriving communities of game developers and publishers, we focus on quality and excellence, and our partners and players are always at the front of everything we do.

Games are not just about gameplay. We believe in the importance of balancing every single aspect of a game that you, as a player will interact with; what you see, how you play and how it makes you feel.

It couldn't be a more exciting time to join us on a new, unannounced game where we are continuing to push both player experience and quality in the creation of this AAA experience.



The Role

We're looking to strengthen our team with a dynamic, enthusiastic, and adaptable Senior Producer to oversee day-to-day game making, team wellbeing, and production processes and pipelines. You'll be a natural problem solver, anticipating challenges before they arise and devising creative solutions to deliver outstanding gaming experiences.

Senior Producers at Stellar are pivotal in driving projects forward. We're looking for a strong communicator who can inspire teams to embrace a collaborative approach throughout the development cycle. This is a highly visible role that requires excellent relationship-building and client management skills, working closely with cross-discipline teams.



As a Senior Producer in the Stellar team, you will:



 - Champion the creative vision of the game, ensuring alignment with overall project goals.

 - Be a market expert that’s up to date with competitor titles and can share in-depth critiques of their strengths and weaknesses. You will understand what looks ‘great’ in our chosen genres and help us to create titles that stand out.

 - Coordinate and manage the production pipeline, ensuring teams are properly aligned and resources are effectively allocated.

 - Collaborate with designers, artists, and engineers to refine game features and execution plans, all while maintaining and promoting the vision of the game.

 - Oversee daily standups, encouraging clear communication and progress tracking.

 - Break our projects down into manageable milestones, demonstrating clearly the expectations and deadlines.

 - Create and maintain project plans, schedules, and progress reports for stakeholders.

 - Be able to quickly identify risks (in your own as well as wider strategies) and establish mitigation strategies throughout the project lifecycle.

 - Act as a point of contact for communication between internal teams and external partners.

 - Facilitate sprint planning and reviews; ensure processes are followed for agile methodologies.

 - Monitor and analyse project metrics to drive continuous improvement and optimise development processes.



Requirements

What we’re looking to see on your CV:

 - Experience in a Senior/Lead Producer role spanning multiple full game lifecycles

 - A strong background in game production, project management, or developer management within the games industry

 - Solid understanding of game development methodologies (Agile, Scrum)

 - Strong people management and relationship-building skills, with experience in client-facing roles and external meetings

 - A sharp eye on the competitive landscape, with a clear understanding of what "good" looks like in current titles

 - Excellent written and verbal communication, with the ability to clearly convey concepts and project status to varied audiences

 - The ability to motivate and align teams across departments

 - Exceptional organisational and multitasking skills

 - Strong creative instincts and a passion for the gaming landscape

 - Familiarity with project management tools (e.g., Jira, Trello, Monday) and game engines (e.g., Unity, Unreal Engine)



Bonus points if you have;

 - Experience in managing hybrid working teams or projects.



Benefits

What you can expect from us:

 - Flexible hybrid working (we work 3 days per week on-sit

source_jobs.title: Associate Technical Director
source_jobs.description:
The Studio:

We are a video game development studio with offices in Guildford, UK and Utrecht, Netherlands. Sitting within the heart of thriving communities of game developers and publishers, we focus on quality and excellence, and our partners and players are always at the front of everything we do.

Games are not just about gameplay. We believe in the importance of balancing every single aspect of a game that you, as a player will interact with; what you see, how you play and how it makes you feel.

It couldn't be a more exciting time to join us on a new, unannounced game where we are continuing to push both player experience and quality in the creation of this AAA experience.



The Role

As an Associate Technical Director, you'll partner closely with our Technical Director to lead and shape the technical direction of our new project. You'll oversee key technical aspects of game development while mentoring and managing a team of engineers, ensuring the game meets its technical goals and pushes boundaries wherever possible.



You'll work across every department, including art, design, production, and external clients, bridging the gap between creative vision and technical execution. We're looking for someone who's just as comfortable being hands-on in the code as they are leading people: a strong mentor who can develop talent and provide clear direction.



If you can combine technical expertise, a passion for games, and strong leadership skills, this could be the role for you.



As our Associate Technical Director, you will:

 - Bridge the gap between creative and technical aspects of development, working closely with art, design, and production

 - Partner with other department leads to shape and implement a clear technical vision for the game

 - Offer pragmatic technical expertise where needed, while empowering your team to find their own solutions

 - Conduct code reviews and debugging, giving constructive feedback to maintain code quality and efficiency

 - Mentor and manage the team, ensuring code quality meets the game's requirements

 - Help build a skilled, motivated technical team through recruitment and talent development

 - Run effective meetings and software/build reviews that encourage active feedback and new ideas

 - Support the Technical Director as needed



Requirements

What we’re looking to see on your CV

 - Proven leadership experience as an Associate Technical Director, Technical Lead, or Lead Engineer, having led at least one full AAA game project from start to finish

 - Strong proficiency in C/C++ and a solid grasp of core game engineering disciplines

 - A track record of building and managing cohesive, high-performing teams

 - Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively across disciplines

 - A genuine passion for making great games and pushing technical boundaries



Bonus points if you have:

 - Previous experience in building large scale, realtime, multiplayer games



Benefits

What you can expect from us:

 - Flexible hybrid working (we work 3 days per week on-site)

 - Holiday Accrual Scheme

 - International AAA game projects;

 - Access to cutting-edge hardware;

 - Discretionary bonus scheme

 - Private Healthcare Plan incl. Dental and Eye care

 - Pension Plan

 - Life Assurance

 - Employee Assistance Programme - Wellness Support

 - Regular Team Incentives and Social Events



We’re on an important journey with our teammates, candidates and industry partners to tackle barriers to equality and promote diversity, inclusion and accessibility across the markets we work in. If you need any support during the interview process or any feedback on our processes, please let us know.



Some things to note before applying

 - We're not able to offer visa sponsorship or relocation assistance for this role, so you'll need to already be based in the UK with the right to work here.

source_jobs.title: Associate Art Director
source_jobs.description:
The Studio

We are a video game development studio with offices in Guildford, UK and Utrecht, Netherlands. Sitting within the heart of thriving communities of game developers and publishers, we focus on quality and excellence, and our partners and players are always at the front of everything we do.



Games are not just about gameplay. We believe in the importance of balancing every single aspect of a game that you, as a player will interact with; what you see, how you play and how it makes you feel.



It couldn’t be a more exciting time to join us as we embark on a new unannounced project, pushing the boundaries of all aspects of the game to create a truly AAA experience.

The Role

Our studio is going from strength to strength and we’re now looking for an Associate Art Director to join us to continue building upon our high-quality art aspirations but also leading a team of high achieving artists. This role sits on a new, unannounced project – so if you’re looking to grow and nurture teams, while shaping the creative vision, this could be the role for you.



You will collaborate with the Art Director and teams across many disciplines including UI, Hard Surface, World, VFX, Animation, Engineering and Production to define and drive the quality in our games.



Alongside leveraging your artistic abilities, you’ll also mentor and manage a variety of artists in different disciplines, providing guidance and support to help them reach their full potential. We’re passionate about career development, and our Associate Art Director will play a key role in the progression of our teams. You’ll be a naturally strong communicator and team leader, who can maintain the balance of artistic creativity and technical constraints to help us deliver visually stunning and immersive game experiences.



As our Associate Art Director, you will:



 - Help to refine and sculpt the artistic vision of the game alongside our Art Director and Head of Creative

 - Collaborate across teams to ensure the art direction complements gameplay

 - Lead and mentor our team of talented artists, giving regular feedback and check-ins on the work produced

 - Work to grow the team’s overall capabilities, maintaining and fostering a healthy culture that means everyone is hungry to be class leading across all disciplines.

 - Ensure we are delivering quality and consistency regarding the artwork we are creating for our projects

 - Communicate and celebrate art progress alongside highlighting risks and concerns looking to optimise the maximum potential of the team





Requirements

What we’re looking to see on your CV:

 - Proven experience as an Associate Art Director or Lead Artist on released game projects within the AAA space.

 - Exceptional portfolio showcasing a strong understanding of artistic fundamentals, including composition, lighting, and visual storytelling.

 - Proficiency in industry-standard art tools (e.g., Photoshop, Maya, Blender, ZBrush, Substance Suite) and game engines (e.g., Unreal Engine).

 - Strong leadership and mentorship skills, with a track record of guiding and developing artistic talent.

 - Excellent communication skills, with the ability to clearly convey artistic vision and provide constructive feedback across multiple disciplines.

 - Ability to balance creative ambition with production realities and technical constraints to deliver high-quality results on time.

 - Strong understanding of the game development process and the intersection of art and technology in game production.



Bonus Points For

 - Experience working on large-scale, open-world, or multiplayer games.

 - Knowledge of advanced rendering techniques and tools (e.g., shaders, lighting systems).

 - Experience in developing art pipelines and workflows that improve productivity and efficiency.

 - Passion for video games and a strong understanding of the latest industry trends in game art and visual styles.



Benefits

What you can expect from us:

 - Flexible hyb

notes: Video game development studio with a strong focus on engineering roles
